note: estimated — run cargo bench for real numbers on your hardware
hardware: M2 MacBook Pro, 16GB RAM, Apple NVMe SSD

benchmarks.note: BTreeMap insert; no disk I/O. Limited by tree rebalancing.

benchmarks.metric: Post-flush random read (1k keys in one SSTable)
benchmarks.note: Sparse index lookup + sequential scan within block. No bloom filter (v1).

benchmarks.metric: Post-flush random read with bloom filter (v2)
benchmarks.note: Bloom filter eliminates disk reads for missing keys (~1% FPR at 10 bits/key).
Sequential read + write bounded by NVMe bandwidth. CPU is not the bottleneck.
At 10 bits/key, k=7 hash functions. Matches theoretical (1 - e^(-kn/m))^k.
All in-memory against hot memtable. Reads hit BTreeMap, no SSTable I/O.
